endpointCoverage.notes: Returns empty array on startup, list of all todos otherwise
Creates todo with title and optional description, returns 201 with id and completed=false
Returns todo or 404 if not found
Partially updates todo fields, returns 200 or 404

storageCheck.notes: Data resets on server restart as specified

errorHandling.notes: Clear error messages with appropriate HTTP status codes for 400, 404, and 500 errors

jsonConsistency.notes: All responses use consistent JSON format with express.json() middleware

idempotencyCheck.notes: PUT fully replaces resource, DELETE on non-existent returns 404 (idempotent)

documentationCheck.notes: API is self-documenting through clear endpoint contracts in implementation-notes.md and spec.md

observations: PUT endpoint requires description field (as per full replace semantics)
PATCH allows partial updates of any field
Title trimming applied on create and update
ID generation uses sequential counter starting at 1
recommendations: None - implementation meets all contract requirements
conclusion: The Todo REST API implementation fully satisfies all acceptance criteria and quality expectations defined in the contract. All 6 CRUD endpoints are functional with proper HTTP semantics, input validation, error handling, and consistent JSON responses.
